title: aster × subgeminatus
intro: Aster × subgeminatus, a sterile hybrid plant derived from the cross between Aster subspicatus and Aster ericoides, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. Traditionally, Aster × subgeminatus has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Aster × subgeminatus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
